name: W. McAlpine Albergotti
motto: Of their own virtues modest men are dumb.
description: His ambition, at first, was to make better grades than any other boy in his class; but he had not been here quite two years before he could be found in his room preparing an "oyster" stew during study hours. "Me" is too modest to speak of his honors, but his career at Clemson has caused all who know him to praise him. Three years after graduation, he can be found at Orangeburg, wiring the subway there.

name: Woodward Allen
motto: His profile was striking, relentless and grand; His manners were gentle, complying and bland.
description: He is one of the natural leaders of his class, having taken an active part in the various phases of college life. However, he believes in sleep, and declares that chapel and reveille were instituted solely to interfere with his morning nap. Occasionally, he may be found in his room studying very hard about—well, anything except his lessons. He attributes his non-studiousness to Alex, who is taking the Civil-(ization) course. "Woodward's" future is hard to foretell; but, in all probability, he will be a botanist.

name: Frederick Rutledge Baker
motto: I'll bind myself to that which once being right, will not be less right when I shrink from it.
description: As a friend, one could not desire a better; as a companion, one could do far worse. Old Vandy rules his battalion with an iron hand, yet his popularity does not suffer. As a dramatic actor, he cannot be equaled by local talent. His successful management of the finances of the Chronicle shows him to be a shrewd businessman. Vandy is so enamored at present that he can think only of her. For him, the finger of Destiny points toward Fame.

name: William Augustus Barnette
motto: Nature listening stood and wondered at the work herself had made.
clubs: Critic, Vice-President, President Palmetto Literary Society
Secretary and Treasurer York County Club, '09
description: This is another York County boy, and he too has "Reddy" for a name. Reddy's early education was obtained at the Rock Hill High School. He spent two years at the Catawba Military Academy, where he received training for the military life at Clemson. Entering Clemson in 1906, he has since been a loyal member of the class of 1910. By a personality which is attractive and good-natured, Barnette has won his way with ease into the companionship and confidence of all who have enjoyed his acquaintance. Besides being a diligent student, he has been alive to other college activities, being a member of the Y.M.C.A., the Literary Society, and the track team. After graduating at Clemson, Barnette will take a university course and then settle down as a veterinarian.

name: Barnett, Willie Dickson
motto: "His mild expression spoke a mind in duty firm, composed, resigned."
clubs: Secretary, Vice-President, and President of Palmetto Society
description: "W.D." rolled down the mountain from Westminster and landed at Clemson in September 1905. From that time, he has kept his eye focused upon a "sheepskin." As a Junior, while climbing the ladder of military fame, he held the reins tight, but his zenith having been reached in his captaincy, he slackened up a bit. Notwithstanding all his military tactics, he was unable to control his section in Senior—he being the sole member. "W.D.'s" future is somewhat uncertain, but he will be either president of the Westminster Cotton Mill or an instructor in German.

name: Baxley, Paul Allen
motto: "Time, I dare thee to discover such a youth and such a lover."
clubs: Prosecuting Critic, Corresponding Secretary, Columbian Society
description: "Paul" or "Juanita" is a quiet little fellow whose only fault is "he ain't no flirt hardly." It is said that Paul was once in love, but we have every reason to doubt it. In spite of all this, he is very fond of reading love stories. "Paul's" intellectual ability is never doubted, high marks being his hobby. We predict for him success as proprietor of the Blackville Dairy Farm.

name: Aaron, Joseph Becker
motto: "When I said that I'd die a bachelor, I never dreamed I'd live to be married."
description: He does as he pleases and takes the confinements. He is far too amiable to allow life to become monotonous, for his favorite pastime is "yelling in the mess hall," and he devoted all meal times in Junior to this avocation. While in the section, he enjoys himself by ruffling "Buck's" temper and declaring his hatred of married life. Although not a studious lad, he has been a class leader. For "Beck," there is safety in predicting a bright and successful future as a distinguished chemist, only to be married by matrimony.

name: Herbert, Festus Bethea
motto: "All I want is a chance."
description: He gave vent to his first wail in the burg of Latta many years ago. Upon reaching the age of discretion, he decided to come to Clemson with the class of '10. As college life was so different from that down on the farm, he took the line of least resistance and pursued straight Agriculture. He is never happier than when Harry pays for olives or buys tobacco. The only military aspirations he ever held vanished when his roommate got a corporalcy. The "Woo Woo" wishes everyone to know that "Bethea is in that." When asked of his future, he replies, "Latta is good enough for Bethea."

name: Boone, Louis Crum
motto: As to the manner born.
clubs: Corporal, First Sergeant, Sergeant-Major, Lieutenant and Adjutant, Cotillion Club
Recording Secretary, Corresponding Secretary, Prosecuting Critic Columbian Society
description: "Daniel" is not closely related to the great pioneer, but seems to come of royal lineage. His graceful manner, his courtly bow, and his valiant heart win friends among both ladies and gentlemen. "Daniel" enjoys his college life, especially becoming interested in the military aspirations of all rats. A hard student, an excellent companion, and a good classmate, Daniel has won the respect, if not friendship, of the whole class, and admiration of the college. May his future continue with the high ideals he now holds.

name: Boykin, Bolivar De Saussure
motto: When I ope' my mouth, let no dog bark.
description: If men are known by their works, how are we to know Bolivar? Don't think him always idle, though, for in some things, he is a heavy man—first comes football. Any time you can't find him on the field, come down into barracks, and you will find him working out some new play. And the ladies! Well, you are right there; that is his long suit. He carries trumps in that game, and can play from his sleeve all day long. Bolivar has many good traits; he is a big, good-natured heart smasher, and is always on time at reveille!

name: McCormick, S. C.
motto: 'Tis glorious to have a giant's strength, but nobler to use it like a man.
clubs: Section marcher in Senior, Junior Electrical Science Club
description: He needs no introduction into the athletic world, but for fear there are some who do not know of his ingenuity as an electrician, this account is written. On account of his genial smile and jolly disposition, he has a host of friends. There have arisen numerous obstacles in his pathway; but, in spite of all, he will graduate in the noble class of '10. He will probably be in charge of the powerhouse at Sandover, a young flourishing city in this State.

name: Brockington, Willie John
motto: Even small things have their value.
description: He is a small but valuable member of our class. Originally, he belonged to the class preceding, but on account of bad health, he was obliged to join the class of '10. He is of a very retiring disposition, but still is fairly well known. His loyalty to the Literary Society and his professed ambition to become a veterinarian are two of his strongest features. He will in all probability live a quiet but useful life down on the farm.

name: Buckner, James Morgan
motto: "If I owned the whole world, then I'd plant it in tobacco."
description: After finishing the Fairfax school, came to Clemson with the sole intention of studying mineralogy. No military for him! As a result of his research work during his Junior year, Buck discovered a combustible, soft flesh-colored substance entirely unknown to science. After consulting all chemists and mineralogists, it was decided to classify and name this mineral "Bucknerite." "Buck" has been a diligent student, a conscientious young man, and a pleasant classmate. Enough for his future can be gotten from above.

name: Burton, George Augustus
motto: "Eternal smiles his emptiness betray."
clubs: Corresponding Secretary and Vice-President of the Calhoun Literary Society
description: "Gus" was born out in the wilds of Arkansas; but, soon after his birth, his parents returned to their native State, and happened to bring little "Gussie" with them. Since then, he has lived at Whitmire, in the northeast corner of Newberry County. His career in college has been a success, interrupted only by an April Fool vacation. Gus has stood well in his classes, but is too fond of boat rides. We hope some day to see him field Secretary of the Chicago Home Industrial Art School.

name: Norman, Ernest Byrd
motto: Let all the world say, 'Here's a man.'
clubs: Winner of four first places in S. I. A. A. Meet, 1909
Executive Com., S. C. Oratorical Association
description: He first came to Clemson in the fall of 1906. His arrival created quite a 'stir' in the college, and the current has not settled yet. Byrd first distinguished himself by rooming on 'Pike.' Then he went in for athletics, where he has made himself famous as the only original human airplane in existence. He makes the hundred yards in 10 flat when not frightened. When afraid he breaks all records. Byrd will be city engineer of Branchville.

name: John, Newton Carothers
motto: Still and quiet, but deeper than you think.
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Bible Class Leader, 1908
description: John is always on time with the goods. Though quiet and unobtrusive, he has opinions of his own, and speaks at the proper time. He is a student of the first class, and in the true sense of the word. He never aspired for military glory, but has ever declared undying love to his musket. Although from Rock Hill, he seldom speaks of Winthrop—it is not known what he writes. As a chemist, John will gain the respect of the world.

name: Francis Worthington Chapman
motto: 'Tis fate that flings the dice; and as one flings, of kings makes peasants, and of peasants kings.
clubs: Junior and Senior Electrical Science Clubs
description: Since coming to Clemson, he has won quite a reputation as a student. His chief faults are buying experience and smashing hearts.

name: Donald Brevard Clayton
motto: I let wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions.
description: "D.B." is like Alexander the Great - not a great fighter (he never was even a corporal), but he is crying for worlds to conquer in the name of Dan Cupid. His love letters "cover Dixie like the dew," and at present he is pushing his conquest into Arkansas. "D.B." has the great distinction of being able to get permits when he wants them. This accounts for his being such a shining light in the young social set at Calhoun.

name: Hugh Robert Clinkscales
motto: "For some who have his secret nature guessed, have found him not too much a priest."
description: "Clink" is a specimen of Fairfield; therefore history and political economy come easy to him. Much to his chagrin, his military aspirations were blighted in Junior. It was found that the atmosphere of Senior privatism was more congenial to his delicate constitution. His qualities as a debater are unlimited, he having met and defeated the renowned and venerable Bob Nickles. His two chief faults were: first, coming to section formation eating the last bite; second, after studying a "Daddy" lesson for three hours, invariably asking every member of the section where the lesson began. Diligence will make his success in life certain.

name: LeRoy Astor Coleman
motto: "Nor cast one longing, lingering look behind."
description: "Roy" is the general name for this youth, though, to close friends, he is "Tige." 'Tis rumored he bears a resemblance to the Tige of Buster Brown fame, but see for yourself. When Roy says he will do a thing, that thing is going to be done—the greatest of virtues. Give him his pipe, a jollier fellow can't be found.

name: William, C. Crum
motto: Plant thou thy feet, as on a stair, And mount right up and on.
description: He is capable of doing anything he desires. His lessons do not bore him very greatly, and he, too, has a jolly time at school. Wherever a little nerve is needed, he is always sent for. Before the footlights or on the sidelines, he always stars. He had his copy of the Denmark Times discontinued on account of the poor editorials - at least that's what we are told. But seriously, he has had a rather enviable college career, and his merry voice will be missed when we have separated. His future is in his own hands, to be as great as he desires.

name: Knox, Easterling
motto: Yet, in his worst pursuits, I ween, Pure hopes of high intent.
description: The subject of this sketch is not half so innocent as he looks. Everybody says he is going to be a rich man; and, if it takes a married man to save the coin, he is going to be second only to Rockefeller. He has given close attention to the study of electricity. Oh, no! That is not all; he is a recognized authority on the culture of strawberries and has been known to carry on scientific investigation in the Horticultural grounds at 2 A.M. By his kind and unobtrusive disposition, he has won many friends during his college days, and it can be well said of him, "The man of great spirit is congenial."

name: Samuel Ernest Evans
motto: Men's evil manners live in brass, their virtues we write in water.
clubs: President, Treasurer, Recording Secretary, Reporting Critic, Presiding Officer 1910, Annual Celebration, Palmetto Literary Society
description: He has always been interested in the affairs of the college and is a veritable information bureau. Next to the Pendleton Guards, he is loyal to the Palmetto Society and presides over its meetings with the skill and grace of a genius. He will win fame as a General Manager for the S.E. Waterman Co. in the Philippines.

name: Gilbert Theodore Floyd
motto: Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil o'er books consumed the midnight oil?
description: This lad from Lake City entered the Preparatory Class in 1905. He is serving five years, instead of seven years as Jacob the brother of Esau did, for his intended. He says nothing is worthwhile that is easy to get. There have been no discordant notes in his career, as his record shows. He spends his vacant hours in the classroom, which accounts for the high marks he makes in steam laboratory. After getting married, "G.T." will circumscribe the globe, using Professor Houston's methods of engineering.

name: Fulmer, Tallye
motto: ’Tis worth that makes the man.
description: He is the same jolly fellow today that he was yesterday, and promises to be as true tomorrow. He has pursued his way with a few interruptions, but at the end will be with the rest of us. One year, he went West for experience. While out there, he became a hero and was quite wildly eulogized—he may even get a Carnegie medal. But of their own merits, modest men are dumb, and doubtless Tallye knows more than we imagine. The future is yet uncertain, but Tallye will make his mark in the electrical world.

name: Gandy, Frank LeRoy
motto: From the haunts of the whippoorwill, came he here, his head with knowledge to fill.
description: Though inclined to be somewhat quiet, likes to talk of old Pee Dee. It’s always summer in and out of Orangeburg when he is at home. To keep him in good humor, don’t rub his head when he gets promoted. Although not a professed ladies’ man, he has a new farm at home. Does this signify some future event of happiness for "Yap"? And I wonder if Section I will be invited? Oh, you "Yap!"

name: Frederick, Benjamin Green
motto: Good nature is the most Godlike commendation of a man.
description: His quiet, unobtrusive disposition and extremely good nature have won him many friends. Freddie will follow his chosen profession and eventually become a shining light in the electrical world.

name: Alfred, Grier
motto: I came, I saw, I loved.
description: Alfred has been too busy composing love letters in poetry to worry the world with his whereabouts. Only in his Senior year has he startled the student body. By his part in the Dramatic Club, he has won undying fame for "Sallee in Frinch." As barracks electrician, he has kept everything light but the mail bag.

name: Henderson, Robert Pratt
motto: It does not pay to worry; things are bound to happen anyway.
description: Pratt is a great fellow in every way; he is over six feet tall. Though he studies but little, he makes good marks and is altogether quite a prominent figure in the "cowboy" section. Pratt's main points are in military affairs and baseball. He can get a permit whenever he desires, if not for baseball purposes, then for anything. Pratt is working hard for his "sheepskin"; when that is obtained, he will either play league baseball or become county veterinarian.

name: Higgins, Victor Barton
motto: He's good in mathematics, can work most any sum; fine in the art of music, but a tune he cannot hum.
description: Responds equally well to "Vic" or "Victor Blue." Military aspirants are glad that this lad connected himself with the band, for he possesses all the traits that go to make a military genius. "Vic" is a recognized tennis player, but he is better known in college by a peculiar Indian whoop that he sends forth when the adjutant reads an interesting notice. He works mathematics for a pastime, and some day he will teach this science at his Alma Mater, if he doesn't decide to survey tunnels through the Blue Ridge Mountains near his home.

name: John Livingstone Hill, Jr.
motto: "And they, I ween, are happiest who defy sunshine or shadow, bright or cloudy sky."
clubs: Winner of two first places in S.I.A.A. Meet, '09
description: He came to college to rest awhile before beginning business for himself. He is bright enough and lucky enough to do as he pleases, and yet make excellent marks. Though a lieutenant in a company, he has the most privileged office in school. While the dull fellows are poring over their books, John is out in society. He is always in good humor, and ready for a good time. Matrimony was once John's chief object; but, since the middle of the year, he has been having visions of great construction work.

name: Walter McLeod Hodge
motto: "No sense of ills to come has he, no care beyond to-day."
description: He hopped from Clarendon County to Clemson in '06. He received his early education at the various county schools, and one year at the Manning Graded School. He has been for four years a faithful member of the chemistry section, greatly aiding that body to uphold its high marks. Why "Froggy" didn't make varsity football is more than we can explain; his "stickability" has never been doubted. 'Tis said he takes German so Professor Doggett will help him in feminine affairs. "Froggy" bids fair to become a tiller of the soil.

name: Onan Augustus Hydrick
motto: He is a man, beloved, respected.
description: He had so many talents, it was a hard matter to decide whether he wanted to be a great athlete, orator, or a leader in class-work. With a little persuasion, he soon decided on a place on the gridiron, and took a course in Civil Engineering to pass away time. He expects to be the leading engineer at Bull Swamp soon.

name: Claude Furman Inman
motto: Few hearts like his with virtue warmed, Few heads with knowledge so informed.
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Recording Secretary, Literary Critic, Vice-President, President Calhoun Society
description: Besides being one of the best disposed and most congenial of the boys in college, he has also been a leading man since his early days under English professors, where he won distinction in scholarship. Though a quiet youth, he cannot refrain from "cracking jokes" at Practical Agriculture, for it seems to revive old recollections. Once he startled the class by announcing that alcohol was the chief food of man. Being such a persistent man, as well as one of ability, we can but predict boundless success as a chemist.

name: Johnson, Henry Stanley
motto: I dare do all that becomes a man; who dares do more is none.
clubs: President, Vice-President, Literary Critic, Prosecuting Critic, Recording Secretary, and Chaplain of the Columbian Society
description: He is one of those fellows who always looks at the worst side first. However, he is an unceasing worker, carries his point at any cost, and does his duty as he sees it. His chief fault is speaking the truth at the wrong moment. He often declares if there ever was a "Jonah," he's the man. His character and high sense of honor have enabled him to go through school respected, if not admired by all. As for Mary's future—well, only a certain girl can tell, and we haven't seen her yet.

name: Kelley, Stanley Ormand
motto: It is not for your silver bright, But for your winsome lady.
description: Like Abraham Lincoln, began his career of usefulness at an early age. He was known to experiment with electricity from his earliest childhood, and the art has never left him—"good" and "very good" are often found on his experiments when they are returned by the professor. Some of these days, "So" will be an engineer of note, but the crowning event of his life was during the encampment at Columbia, where he found his affinity. He is now enjoying the sunshine of eternal bliss and has rented a box at the post office to keep from imposing upon the cadet mail carriers. "So" is an ardent member of the Y.M.C.A., and when he eats chicken in barracks, he is always so polite that he never inquires where the chicken came from.

name: Laurent, L. Laroche
motto: "This was the noblest Roman of them all."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Dancing Club, '09
description: With his light hair and peculiar brogue, has won his way into the hearts of all he knows. He is in a course by himself and therefore has more studying to do than we of larger sections. He often talks and talks without saying anything bad about anyone—a remarkable ability. His business nature was shown in various ways, as can be seen below. He is evidently very smart, as he makes the best mark in his section. We have no fear that he will have trouble in securing a partner to take the course of life, and that they, two, will have no difficulties insurmountable.

name: Perry, Earle Lee
motto: "A man of cheerful yesterdays and confident tomorrows."
description: This young man of charming manner and beaming countenance entered Sophomore in '07 and was early attracted by the desire to earn military fame. Hence, he became a sergeant in his Junior year, where nobody could dispute his acting thoroughly in accordance with all military rules and regulations. Many were the privates who ceased to smile and raise their hands in ranks, at reveille or while marching to breakfast. It is rumored that Perry found a Queen in Columbia during the State Fair in that city, to whom he willingly gave all his newly acquired possessions as well as his heart.

name: William, Jordan Marshall
motto: Complacency and truth, and manly sweetness dwell ever on his tongue and smooth his thoughts.
clubs: President, Vice-President, Recording Secretary, Chaplain Calhoun Society
description: His chief characteristics are his nose and fascinating manner. The only changeable things about him seem to be his voice and his religion. He is a great society man (literary, of course) and is exceedingly proud of his military achievements. His redeeming features are his high sense of honor, his good nature, and his ability to get work out of everybody—except himself. "Billy" will return to the farm eventually, marry, and devote his time to raising "Sweet Williams" and "Berkshires."

name: Edgar, Charles Martin
motto: Boys, says he, 'I've got to have a mate.'
description: Here our nicknames ran out, and this jovial lad is called only Martin. He is a great lover of qualitative analysis but more so of quantitative. Here in the laboratory, he spends his pleasantest days. We wonder why he did not take the chemistry course. His experiments have ended in wonder and awe; as yet the lab is still standing and no one yet seriously injured. As for the study of Forestry—well, "Ed" is second only to Pinchot. Perhaps we "horse laugh" at his jokes, perhaps we don't; but let him have his way for fear of "bodily harm."

name: Charles Francis Middleton
motto: An honest man is the noblest work of God.
clubs: Critic, Secretary, President Columbian Literary Society
description: His early training being over-balanced on the society side, he found it expedient to enter the Preparatory Class at Clemson. His kindly expression and gentle disposition have caused him to be universally liked. While somewhat averse to any work, he has made fairly good records in college. His manners will win him fame, second only to that of Lord Chesterfield.

name: John Daniel Murray
motto: Least, but not last.
description: On his arrival at college, he weighed only sixty-nine pounds and was under five feet; in fact, the signal corps seemed too large for him. John D.'s progress in college has been along all lines, so that today he is a respectable-looking fellow. The small are ever the wittiest, and as a rule the smartest. On account of his customary laugh and his ability for spinning yarns, D. has been rather popular. He likes the college so well that he might take a post-graduate course.

name: Albert, Calhoun McDavid
motto:
"Would not we shatter it to bits—and then
Remold it nearer to heart's desire."
description: He is a great lover of the beautiful, and spends his lonesome moments with his favorite music. One of Bert's favorite amusements is waking the boys for reveille by blowing his own horn; however, we can forgive him when he lulls us to sleep with the sweet strains of "Taps." After launching on an engineering career, Bert will use both music and science to persuade a certain little maid.

name: Addison, Alexander McKeown
motto:
"To those who talk and talk,
This proverb should appeal,
The steam that blows the whistle,
Never turns the wheel."
description: Mac is a loyal member of the famous Pendleton Guards and spent the entire summer of '08 studying in order to maintain his place among the '10's. When Mac is not found writing letters, he is most likely somewhere boosting Chester, his home town. His college career has been bright, useful and altogether successful, and his future can be no less so.

name: Colin, McLaurin
motto: Who mixed reason with pleasure and wisdom with mirth.
description: Being of Scotch descent, minds his own affairs exclusively. Although of a quiet and retiring disposition, he is well known and is a good companion. He is true to his friends and speaks no ill of his enemies. In the military line, "Buster" is all that could be desired of an officer. In his early days at college, he gave promise of becoming a great orator, but for some reason, "Buster" has not developed his ability as a speaker. Before him lies a career of trust and honor—we would not say fame.

name: Robert, Edward Nickles
motto: My oratory will make me famous.
clubs: Class Lawyer in Junior and Senior
President, Literary Critic, Secretary, Censor, Prosecuting Critic, Reporting Critic, Sergeant-at-Arms Palmetto Literary Society
description: It is a rare occurrence if a body of college men, so large as this class, does not have an aspiring lawyer, and we are no exception; for we are burdened with this fellow who answers to "Bob" or "Robert." Since he has performed oratorical gymnastics of astounding eloquence and grace, he will attain distinction like unto Cicero, Webster or Hayne. But really, "Bob" has had a remarkably successful career, for he has succeeded in bluffing all the faculty except the ever-alert professor of geology. For "Bob" is predicted a boundless success as lawyer, and authority on "The Lien Law."

name: Kollock, Sarius Olin Pegues
motto: He was a man, take him for all in all; I shall not look on his like again.
clubs: Corporal, First Sergeant, Color-Sergeant, Sergeant-major, Captain and Adjutant
description: He has an individual personality and air that puts him above most men. With a will of his own, and a disregard of public opinion, "Pig" has done his duty faithfully. There are latent powers in him that know no bounds, if only awakened.

name: Charleston, Edward Hall Pinckney
motto: He pleases when he kisses, and kisses when he pleases.
description: As soon as Ed gets his diploma, he will return to the little girl (or girls) who awaits him by the sea, and they will live happily ever afterward.

name: Hervey, D. Plenge
motto: Few words, much sleep, and plenty to eat, make a man morally deep.
description: What this lad intends to do, very few seem to know. He is taking the electrical course and is good in all of his work—whenever he works. Plenge is a "battery" product, but he never says enough for anyone to discover this fact. His classmates say he is in love, but he stoutly denies the accusation. He never worries his roommates by keeping the light on too late. Plenge is a staunch advocate of the saying, "Eat while you sleep or sleep while you eat."

name: John, Saville Pyatt, Jr.
motto: He says what he means, and means what he says.
description: "Hen" or "Chick" is appropriately nicknamed; ordinarily he is as peaceful as can be, but ruffle his feathers and you have a "sitting hen." His outstanding characteristic is a too great admiration for his professors and his readiness to defend them. As the Winyah Graded School could not give "Chick" the desired education, he entered "Prep" here many moons ago. His greatest disappointment at college was the rejection of his application for Corporal at the end of his Freshman year. "Hen" may come back and take a post-graduate course in Dairying and Forestry. His fame as a landscape gardener will soon become known to the world.

name: Reaves, Harry Lucius
motto: "Shall I not take mine ease in mine inn?"
description: Harry has always been popular, jolly, and successful in class work. The sore point with him is the position in military affairs he received in Senior year. The agricultural section owes much to the scientific research work of "Baby." The friendship of Harry and "Puss" Bethea is like that of Damon and Pythias. Harry will probably spend most of his time in keeping track of Bethea, though he can accomplish whatever he wishes.

name: Reid, Johnson Crayton
motto: "I have been merry twice, and once ere now."
description: Johnson is one of those jolly fellows who believe in having a good time whatever else you do. His greatest achievement along any line in college is being the author of the "Bowery," the bottom floor of new barracks. Here he has remained ever since. The "Bowery" bunch have had some good times studying together. With his pipe and an easy chair, Johnson is content to dream dreams in the smoke; however, he is not an all-around dreamer.

name: J. McLaurin, L. Ridgill
motto: Sometimes from the humblest place, there rises one to rule the race.
description: Ridgill has no pet names, but has always been called by his surname in college. He cast his lot with the "hay seeds" and is true to that section. Nothing great has disturbed his career, and his quest for knowledge has been uninterrupted. He is not a man who pushes himself forward, nor yet so very retiring. We do not believe Ridgill has yet realized his capabilities; but, when he does, a successful career is open to him.

name: Robbs, Charlie Macbeth
motto: With thee are silent fame, forgotten arts and wisdom disappeared.
description: "Charlie" is known by the students to be an all-round man and a hero on the football field. He received his education at the Spartanburg and Gaffney graded schools. At Clemson, he has pursued the Civil Engineering course with a remarkable degree of success. As captain of the "Tigers," he has won world-wide renown. "Charlie" will probably devote his time to the building up of his home town.

name: Roberts, Charles Pressley Jr.
motto: Loyal-hearted, strong of mind, a finer boy nowhere you'll find.
clubs: Sergeant, President, Critic, Recording Secretary, Corresponding Secretary, Chaplain and Sergeant-at-Arms of Calhoun Literary Society
description: The professor of civil engineering thinks that some day, "C. P." will be a great engineer; but his classmates are prone to believe that he will get married and go to farming, or drawing plans for skyscrapers and log cabins.

name: Robinson, William Alexander
motto: A glad heart maketh a cheerful countenance.
description: "Cherry" is all that his name implies, a red-cheeked, good-natured fellow. "Alex" reached the height of his ambition when he was put in the band because his roommate could wield a baton. Then, too, he was a very good cymbal beater, and is not bad on alto. "Cherry" is not a dull boy by any means, for he gets along well without studying. He will return to Easley, and, if he doesn't become a minister, will be county engineer.

name: Rogers, Louis David
motto: Be to his virtues a little kind, Be to his faults a little blind.
description: The smallest boy that entered Clemson in '06 was afterwards found to be "Kid" Rogers. Being too small for a regular soldier, he was assigned to the signal corps. "Kid" has never had any trouble in making his class—the small are ever great. He is another chemist that the class of '10 is turning out. Rogers has never had any military aspirations but has devoted his time to studying and the other pleasures of college life.

name: Ryan, Gerald Duncan
motto: How blest is he who crowns in shades like these, A youth of labor with an age of ease.
description: "Gerald" entered college in the fall of '05, soon attaining the distinction of section marcher. Upon entering the Sophomore class, he decided to become an electrical engineer. His fame has already begun to spread among his classmates, who predict a brilliant future for him. He is particularly famous for his freehand sketches of connections and allowing others to work up data for and with him. At present, he is forming a partnership with a gentleman who expects to adjust all instruments to an accuracy of one thousandth of one percent. Ryan's expectations are thus brightened and assured for the future.

name: Salley, Thomas Raysor
motto: How hard their lot, who never won nor lost.
description: From the 'Land of the Sky,' he came to Clemson, and proceeded to elevate himself to the office of Senior captain. On account of his roving disposition, acquired in early life, he was retired in November and permanently located in room 372 until June. Raysor's business ability assures a promising future for him.

name: Seabrook, William Edings
motto: The cheerfulness of some people is maddening.
description: He is a Georgian, born and bred, though he has been in South Carolina so long that he is generally regarded as a naturalized citizen. However, he sometimes betrays his birthplace by outbursts of loyalty to the 'Empire State of the South.' Bill is noted as an orator, having once made an address to some 'old boys' when he was a 'rat.' Bill's good-natured smile and his general appearance have won for him many lifelong friends. He will someday startle the world by inventing a transit that can be used while the operator reads a book under some convenient tree.

name: Shuler, John Jacob Dreher
motto: Slow, but sure.
description: He is a specimen from Lexington County. Since then, he has, with undaunted determination, been steadily climbing upward with the "Hay Seeds." Quite often during his rat-hood days he could be found on hall number ten, singing the laundry list for soda crackers, hence his name, "Polly." His vacant hours are spent in discussing Prof. Harper's theory of Agriculture. "Polly" is as regular in his movements as the sun; only once has he had an eclipse, that was one day he failed to go to drill. "Polly's" future, he keeps to himself, but suffice it to say he's bound to make a success in time.

name: Shuler, Karl Bernard
motto: Words without thoughts never to heaven go.
description: Great excitement reigned when the city of Irmo learned of the "Bird's" departure for Clemson. Since his arrival at college, he has had no time for anything save attending formations and studying Political Economy. He is so busy he seldom speaks. Sometimes he is called "Polly," but that worthy bird speaks all the time, not so with this one. In this youth's career, there has been no discordant note, but a peaceful uneventful life. His future bids the same.

name: Sharon, S. C.
motto: "For some who have his secret nature guessed, Have found him not too much a priest."
clubs: Junior and Senior Electrical Science Clubs
description: "Red" is the name that clung to this lad long ere he came to Clemson; by this name only is he generally known. He is a most diligent student, spending one-tenth of his time in deeper study, the remainder he uses for discourses on the agricultural students—he himself being an electrical. However, "Red" is a jolly, good-natured fellow, and is not lost for friends in school. He is most gallant as a ladies' man. Were it not for this fact, we should feel safe in predicting a great career as an electrician for him. He can amply supply the lights for Sharon, in addition to his other duties.

name: Robert Millen Simpson
motto: "For he is a jolly good fellow, whom no one can deny."
clubs: Corresponding Secretary, Treasurer, Vice-President Calhoun Literary Society
description: "Simp" hails from Chester, and is no exception to the reputation of that county; for there isn't a more obliging or better-disposed boy in college. But the association with "Mac," his roommate, has been very detrimental to himself, as he now persistently absents himself from Sunday school, and becomes prominent at Bible classes by continued absences. In reality, however, he is a "very good fellow," who takes life easy. The greatest problem that ever confronted "Simp," came when he had to decide whether or not he would take German. Some day he will be famous as a chemist.

name: Stephenson, John Trantham
motto: "With never a care for the days to come, he jogs along."
description: He is a fair representative of Kershaw, where he received his early training a few years ago. He came to Clemson in 1906 hoping to become a great electrician. Several times he has been tempted to leave the path he chose for a more alluring cause, but he is still in pursuit of his studies. He is noted for never troubling trouble till trouble troubles him. His character is as steady as his motions, never suffering any inconvenience. "Steve" never shirks his duty, except when room orderly. His future—well, get someone who knows to tell you.

name: Sullivan, Stark Bellotte
motto: "Kind hearts are more than coronets."
description: He began his illustrious career in Anderson some few years back. With all the educational advantages that city offers, "Stool" felt called to Clemson. To those who know him, he is a jolly, true friend; to those who don't, he is a nice fellow. His military training was received in the Signal Corps, hence his aspirations were abolished with that worthy organization. The future of the honorable "S. Ballew" promises one of great magnitude.

name: Summers, Lucius Welborne
motto: It is better to be born lucky than with great riches.
clubs: Delegate to Convention at Columbus, O., '09
description: Received his youthful training at Springfield; maybe this accounts for his position in military affairs. However that may be, he has always been a leader of his class. It is a mystery to his classmates how he can do so little studying, and then come out with excellent marks. His manners are such that he easily makes friends, and, maybe, he has made friends with the professors. Under his guidance, the "cowboy" section has established an enviable reputation. "Chunk" has always made a hit with the ladies; but, if matrimony doesn't prevent, he will finish a university course in his chosen profession.

name: Tarbox, Frank Green Jr.
motto: Know thy work and do it.
description: Is small, quiet; yet an active member of the Senior Class. Coming here in '05, it was thought necessary to put "Box" in the Signal Corps. Here he remained till that organization disbanded, winning military fame for himself. You can find him any time of day or night in his room studying or reading scientific literature. "Box" is bound to make his mark in the world.

name: Thornhill, Edwin Jesse
motto:
"Oh! blessed with temper whose unclouded ray
Will make tomorrow as cheerful as to-day."
description: Though rather small, is still a prominent figure in school. His business ability asserted itself in the store which he had. He is loved and respected, especially by those who have not yet paid up. His genial smile and jovial nature have made him friends where others would have failed. Had he started athletics upon entering school, he would doubtless be wearing the block "C," as his work on scrub football was excellent. Do not for a moment forget Eddie was one of the loyal Pendleton guards. From present indications, Eddie will return home and enlighten the town as to successful business methods.

name: Townsend, Charles Pinckney
motto:
"Thou hast been called, O Sleep! the friend of woe;
But 'tis the happy that have called thee so."
description: "Judge" obtained his title in quite an unusual manner. While he was a rat, he was asked his name, and made the following sage-like remark, "Why, I'm Judge Townsend's boy!" "Judge" has made quite a reputation for himself among his classmates; and, putting all joking aside, we expect to see "Judge" at the top of the ladder some day, an example to Clemson men and a pride of '10. However, we are compelled to admit that "Judge" is a fairly good hand at making short circuits. He doesn't make a great many, but those that he has made have never been surpassed in the annals of Clemson College.

name: Trott, Charles Hard
motto: Think not silence the wisdom of fools.
description: He is another of the brilliant civil engineers to graduate this year. His career is at present likely to be diverted for the stage, having won great renown as "Harris" in the Dramatic Club. Of a good, humorous nature, attending to his own affairs, avoiding the ladies, with an occasional argument with professors, Charlie has gone through college with all the friends he needs. The merry twinkle of his black eyes often speaks when his tongue does not.

name: Twiggs, Henry Cummings
motto: My girl and my pipe, and the world is all right.
clubs: Corporal, First Sergeant, Palmetto Literary Society
description: He is by birth a Georgian but emigrated to South Carolina in his childhood, where he has ever been happy and contented. "Runt" has won quite a reputation in college, both as a writer and as an impersonator. The first distinction was won by his excellent contributions to the *Chronicle*, and the second by his successful takeoff of Prof. S.B. Earle at the Glee Club entertainment. Henry will no doubt devote his time to editing a magazine or to civil engineering in a calm country where the wind won't blow the fire out of his pipe.

name: Warner, Virgil Lee
motto: In me there dwells no greatness, save it be some far off touch of greatness to know well I am not great.
description: He is a hard student and spends his vacant hours assiduously reading bulletins, yearbooks, etc. He believes in being neither seen nor heard, which accounts for his genius remaining in obscurity. "Snipe" will follow his chosen profession and someday will win fame as an agricultural chemist.

name: Webb, Lucius Durant
motto: 'Tis well to have opinions of your own.
description: Lucius hasn't attracted any attention as a great athlete, but he has won quite a reputation as a slide trombone artist. No debating society has his name on its roll, but nevertheless, he has wonderful ability as a debater. He has never been known to give over in any argument, and he chooses such subjects as "'Tis better to have an empty pocket and a full stomach than a full pocket and an empty stomach."

name: Walter Pinckney White
motto: None knew thee but to love thee. None named thee but to praise.
clubs: Manager and Coach Junior Football Team (class champions, '09)
Winner of first medal for shot put and second medal for high jump at S.I.A.A. Meet in Nashville
description: He has gained distinction in all phases of college life. His jolly disposition is ruled by a will and determination that speaks of manhood. As a football hero, his name is foremost on the lips of every student. His college career has been one of success, and there is no doubt that his life career will be just as successful.

name: James McFadden Wylie
motto: Though matrimony has some pains, Celibacy has few pleasures.
description: He is a true son of old Ireland, as his head readily tells, and his wit implies. Leaving college for a while, he became a firm believer in the above verse. This year he returned to complete his course in civil engineering. He speaks what he thinks and has his own opinion. In him you find a friend indeed when he expresses friendship. He bids fair to be an engineer of no small renown.
